per_ac_views.ac_statement: Given a user navigates to the application. When the page loads. Then a form is displayed with input fields for bug title and bug description, and a submit button.

tests_exercising_this_ac.description: Form with title and description fields renders on page load

per_ac_views.implementation_notes: Frontend presentation layer (CreateBugForm component). Forces: field IDs/selectors, button label, form structure.

per_ac_views.ac_statement: Given the create bug form is displayed. When the user enters text into the title field and the description field. Then the entered text is visible and retained in the respective fields.

tests_exercising_this_ac.description: User can enter and retain text in form title and description fields

per_ac_views.implementation_notes: Frontend state management. Forces: controlled vs. uncontrolled inputs, state shape.

per_ac_views.ac_statement: Given the user has entered a title and description in the form. When the user clicks the submit button. Then the form submission is initiated.

tests_exercising_this_ac.description: Form submission is initiated when submit button is clicked

per_ac_views.implementation_notes: Form submission event handling. Forces: request method, URL, content-type, failure handling.

per_ac_views.ac_statement: Given a user submits the form with title "Login button broken" and description "Users cannot click the login button on mobile devices". When the form submission completes successfully. Then a bug is created in the system with title "Login button broken" and description "Users cannot click the login button on mobile devices".

tests_exercising_this_ac.description: Bug is created in database with submitted title and description

per_ac_views.implementation_notes: First end-to-end backend test. BugService.create_bug() and BugRepository.create(). Forces: request schema (JSON), validation rules, transaction atomicity.

per_ac_views.ac_statement: Given a bug is created via form submission. When the creation completes. Then the bug is assigned a unique numeric identifier.

tests_exercising_this_ac.description: Created bug is assigned a unique numeric identifier

per_ac_views.implementation_notes: Database auto-increment ID generation. BugRepository and BugService transaction logic. Forces: auto-increment vs UUID decision, ID return to frontend, enables T7 detail page URL.

per_ac_views.ac_statement: Given a bug is created via form submission. When the creation completes. Then the bug's status is set to "open".

tests_exercising_this_ac.description: Created bug's status is initialized to open

per_ac_views.implementation_notes: Bug domain model status enum. BugService transaction logic. Forces: enum definition location, recognized status values, invalid status rejection at write time.

per_ac_views.ac_statement: Given a user submits the create bug form. When the bug is created successfully. Then the user's browser navigates to the bug's detail page at URL /bugs/{bug-id}, where {bug-id} is the numeric identifier of the created bug.

tests_exercising_this_ac.description: User is navigated to bug detail page with correct bug ID in URL after successful submission

per_ac_views.implementation_notes: Frontend router receives bug ID from API response. Final integration. Forces: bug ID field name in response, router state management pattern.
